role: This file is the host overlay surface that presents the live channel fabric as a DNA-rendered hub-and-spoke graph, opened by a global window event and closed via standard overlay controls.
capabilities: Renders a modal overlay for the channel fabric
Loads live channel data from channelStore
Opens on custom window event channels:open
Closes via Escape key, scrim click, and close button
Fetches and caches the channel-view DNA archetype
Normalizes channel members object to id array for the DNA adapter
Delegates graph layout and SVG rendering to DNA channelGraph/hubNetwork/renderArchetype
Shows loading, error-with-retry, and empty states
Degrades gracefully when DNA or archetype is unavailable
substance_why: It implements the full overlay lifecycle, live data integration, error/empty states, and degrade-clean rendering, with only the graph drawing itself delegated to DNA.
interactions_present: channels:open window event opens the overlay
interactions_missing: No in-component trigger/button to open the overlay
No focus trap or focus restoration on open/close
No scroll lock or body overflow management
No graph-node or channel selection interactions
No loading indicator while fetching the DNA archetype
No explicit handling for DNA adapter/runtime errors beyond a generic catch
No responsive resize/redraw behavior for the graph
performance_risks: Rebuilds and remounts the entire DNA SVG graph whenever the channels reference changes
No memoization of normalized channel records or graph device output
Fetches /dna/layouts.json repeatedly if the archetype cache is not populated
Potential memory leaks from DNA-attached SVG listeners not explicitly cleaned up
reuse_why: The overlay host pattern, open/close lifecycle, and degrade-clean error states are worth keeping, but the DNA global integration and members-normalization hack should be replaced by a first-class graph renderer.
decomposition_hints: Split the overlay shell (scrim, header, close controls, aria) from the graph renderer (data adapter + visual mount)
Split the channel-to-graph data adapter (members normalization, record shaping) into a separate module
Split the DNA archetype loader/cache into a reusable hook or service
